emotional_core: The tools were different — the curiosity was the same: poetry and data science are not opposites, and pretending they were cost something real.
visual_metaphor: A prism splitting white light into full spectrum colour — both the analytical and the lyrical are present in the same beam; poetry doesn't add colour, it reveals what was already there
key_quotes: Poetry dips its fingers in every colour not because poets are ambitious. Because human experience refuses to be sorted.
The door doesn't need to be wide. A crack is enough.
Facts tell you what happened. Poetry tells you what stayed behind after it happened.

story_frames.visual: Hands on a dark keyboard, terminal glow the only light source — late, intimate, private
story_frames.text: Numbers didn't prepare me for this.

story_frames.visual: Open book on a sunlit desk, a prism of light breaking across the pages into full colour spectrum
Close-up of burnt toast being scraped over a white sink, morning light, hyper-specific and ordinary
Abstract dark canvas with vivid paint strokes mid-burst — red, blue, gold — unresolved and alive
story_frames.text: Every colour. Even the dark ones.

story_frames.visual: A lone figure at the edge of a misty lake at dawn — small in the frame, completely still
A hand holding a paperback on a commuter train, city blurred behind the window in motion
A door ajar — warm amber light bleeding through a narrow crack into an otherwise dark hallway
